# Create an alias suffix for a scope

Creating aliases for project scopes lets scope administrators manage their own aliases, seeing only the resources that belong to their projects.

When you create an alias in a project scope, you must create suffixes for the containing org scope and the project scope. Once you create the suffixes, Boundary appends them to any aliases you create in the project scope. You can reuse alias values across scopes because the suffixes make them globally unique values.

For example, you might have an org scope for your engineering team, and project scopes for the front end and back end teams. You could create a global alias called `postgres-db` with an org suffix `eng` and project suffixes `front-end` and `back-end`.

Org suffixes must be globally unique. Project suffixes must be unique within the org the project resides in.

## Permissions

@include '/suffixes/permissions.mdx'

## Create the suffix

Complete the following steps to create a suffix for a scope:

<Tabs>
<Tab heading="UI" group="ui">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Select **Orgs** in the navigation pane, and then select the org or project scope you want to create a suffix for.
1. Click **Create alias suffix**.
1. In the **Alias suffix** field, type a unique value for the suffix. Boundary will append this suffix to any aliases you create within the org or project scope.
1. Click **Save**.

</Tab>

<Tab heading="CLI" group="cli">

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command to create an alias suffix:

   ```shell-session
   $ boundary scopes set-alias-suffix \
       -id o_1234567890 \
       -alias-suffix org1
   ```

   You must provide the the following attributes when you create a suffix:

   @include 'scopes/set-alias-suffix.mdx'


</Tab>
</Tabs>

## Next steps

Once you have created a suffix for an org or project scope, you can [Create a target alias](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/create-target-alias) for targets.

## More information

- For more information about aliases, refer to [Overview of aliases](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration).
- To edit or delete existing suffixes, refer to [Manage suffixes](/boundary/docs/targets/configuration/manage-suffix).
